mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier discounting and lower financing stress, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal turns negative when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent threshold marks deteriorating household income and weaker demand, which should reduce corporate revenue expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes of UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not negative in the triggered set.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth and PAYEMS growth move together, the signal indicates broad-based consumption support and payroll income expansion, which should lift near-term equity returns. The effect is not expected when sales rise but payrolls fall.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive shifts in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20d SPX returns, or if the interaction term has no incremental explanatory power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and realized supply-side momentum, which should support earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month changes in INDPRO are not followed by higher 60d SPX returns than the unconditional average, or if the sign reverses in more than one rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below a recent baseline reflects weakening housing demand and a broader slowdown in cyclical spending, which should pressure equities over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if the triggered average return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth are simultaneously positive, real output and nominal consumer spending are confirming each other, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails if one series improves while the other contracts.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not beat single-series benchmarks for predicting 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction coefficient is unstable across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive rate regime, a high FEDFUNDS level compresses risk appetite and raises discount rates, so equity returns over the next week should be negative. The effect is strongest once policy is above a clearly tight threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS above 4.5% is not associated with negative 5d SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in more than 55% of threshold-crossing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime, stable PAYEMS combined with low UNRATE signals labor-market resilience and supports near-term risk taking, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal weakens if payroll gains are accompanied by rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE combination does not improve prediction of 20d SPX returns versus PAYEMS alone, or if positive returns do not dominate when UNRATE is flat to lower.
